# The Research Gap - Field Notes

**Issue #2 — Retrieval and Collective Effects**

---

## This Week's Signal

The dominant thread this week is consequence, not capability.

---

## Primary Signals

### 1. Evaluating Collective Behaviour of Hundreds of LLM Agents

**arXiv:2602.16662** | [Link](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.16662v1)

**Technical summary:** The framework encodes LLM-generated strategies as executable algorithms, enabling pre-deployment inspection at scale.

**Why it matters:** More recent LLMs tend to produce worse collective outcomes than older models.

---

### 2. Cognitive Debt in AI-Augmented Research

**arXiv:osf:8hbp9_v1** | [Link](https://osf.io/preprints/psyarxiv/8hbp9_v1/)

**Technical summary:** The paper formalizes cognitive debt as cumulative cost from chronic passive reliance on AI systems.

**Why it matters:** The distinction between active and passive AI use is operationally meaningful.

---

## Supporting Evidence

- **Markerless Robot Detection** (arXiv:2602.16308): Replaces fiducial markers with learned 6D pose estimation.
- **Multi-Agent Meta-Advisor for UAV Fleet** (arXiv:2602.16345): Learns a meta-policy across tasks to guide UAV exploration.

---

## Pattern Watch

**Retrieval as a replacement for stateful pipelines.** Two papers this week independently arrive at the same structural move: replacing brittle stateful processes with retrieval over historical or reference data.

**Collective and population-level effects are becoming a first-class research concern.** Three papers this week shift the unit of analysis from individual model performance to population or system-level outcomes.

---
